name: competitive-verifier

metadata.name: competitive-verifier
metadata.summary: Verifier for libraries of competitive programming

documentation:
# competitive-verifier

[![Actions Status](https://github.com/competitive-verifier/competitive-verifier/actions/workflows/verify.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/competitive-verifier/competitive-verifier/actions) [![GitHub Pages](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=GitHub+Pages&message=+&color=brightgreen&logo=github)](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/competitive-verifier)](https://pypi.org/project/competitive-verifier/)

The library is inspired by [online-judge-tools/verification-helper](https://github.com/online-judge-tools/verification-helper).

- [Getting Started](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/installer.html) / [日本語](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/installer.ja.html)
- [Reference](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/document.html) / [日本語](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/document.ja.html)
- [DESIGN(日本語)](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/DESIGN)


## Get started

### Use in GitHub Actions

See [GitHub Pages](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/installer.html).
[日本語](https://competitive-verifier.github.io/competitive-verifier/installer.ja.html)

### Use in local

#### Install(local)

Needs Python 3.9 or greater.

```sh
pip install competitive-verifier
```

Or

```sh
pip install git+https://github.com/competitive-verifier/competitive-verifier.git@latest
```

**Migrate from verification-helper**

Run this script.

```sh
competitive-verifier migrate
```

## Development for contributors

```sh
pip install -U poetry
poetry install

# test
poetry run pytest

# format
poetry run poe format
```

functions.name: onlinejudge._implementation.utils.get_direct_children_text
documentation:
get_direct_children_text collects the text which are direct children of the given tag. For example, this returns "A - Hello world " for a tag `

A - Hello world Editorial

`.

functions.name: onlinejudge._implementation.utils.parse_content
documentation:
parse_content convert a tag to a string with interpretting `
` and ignoring other tags. :::{seealso} :::

functions.name: onlinejudge._implementation.utils.textfile
documentation:
textfile convert a string s to the "text file" defined in POSIX

:::{deprecated} 10.1.0 Use :func:`format_sample_case` instead.
:::

functions.name: onlinejudge._implementation.utils.format_sample_case
documentation:
format_sample_case convert a string s to a good form as a sample case.

A good form means that, it use LR instead of CRLF, it has the trailing newline, and it has no superfluous whitespaces.

functions.name: onlinejudge._implementation.utils.normpath
documentation: We should use this instead of posixpath.normpath
posixpath.normpath doesn't collapse a leading duplicated slashes. see: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7816818/why-doesnt-os-normpath-collapse-a-leading-double-slash

functions.name: onlinejudge._implementation.utils.request
documentation:
`request()` is a wrapper of the `requests` package with logging.

There is a way to bring logs from `requests` via `urllib3`, but we don't use it, because it's not very intended feature ant not very customizable. See https://2.python-requests.org/en/master/api/#api-changes

documentation: the module for those who make programs using online-judge-tools as a library

functions.name: onlinejudge.utils.get_default_session
documentation:
get the default session used in online-judge-tools

```
note

cookie is not saved to disk by default. check 

note

the user agent is the default of the requests library.
```
NOTE: this function should not be used internally; if used, we may make bugs that given sessions are ignored

functions.name: onlinejudge.utils.with_cookiejar
documentation:
---
param path: a path to the file to store cookies. the default cookiejar is used if
  {py:class}`None`
param session: the session to set a cookiejar
---
NOTE: This function ignores `discard` and `expires` attributes because some cookies disappear if considering them. This is for mainly Kagamiz Contest System.

methods.name: competitive_verifier.verify.split_state.SplitState.split
documentation:
Split list


Args:
    lst (list[T]): Target list

Returns:
    list[T]: Splited list

Example:
    state = SplitState(size=3, index=0)
    assert state.split([0, 1, 2, 3, 4]) == [0]
    state = SplitState(size=3, index=1)
    assert state.split([0, 1, 2, 3, 4]) == [1, 2]
    state = SplitState(size=3, index=2)
    assert state.split([0, 1, 2, 3, 4]) == [3, 4]
    state = SplitState(size=6, index=4)
    assert state.split([0, 1, 2, 3, 4]) == [4]
    state = SplitState(size=6, index=5)
    assert state.split([0, 1, 2, 3, 4]) == []

functions.name: competitive_verifier.oj.verify.utils.glob_with_predicate
documentation:
glob_with_basename iterates files whose basenames satisfy the predicate.

This function ignores hidden directories and hidden files, whose names start with dot `.` letter.

the module for AtCoder (<https://atcoder.jp/>)

There are some useful endpoints:

https://atcoder.jp/contests/abc001/standings/json

https://atcoder.jp/users/chokudai/history/json

There is an unofficial API https://github.com/kenkoooo/AtCoderProblems

Some methods not inherited from classes  may be modified in future, because the specification is not fixed yet.

functions.name: onlinejudge.service.atcoder._request

This is a workaround. AtCoder's servers sometime fail to send "Content-Type" field.
see https://github.com/kmyk/online-judge-tools/issues/28 and https://github.com/kmyk/online-judge-tools/issues/232

methods.name: onlinejudge.service.atcoder.AtCoderContest.iterate_submission_data_where
documentation:
---
note: If you use certain combination of options, then the results may not correct
  when there are new submissions while crawling.
param lang: must be one of `ja`, `en`
param language_id: is used as the value of `f.Language` query parameter
param me: use the `.../submissions/me` page instead of `.../submission`
param order: must be one of `created`, `score`, `source_length`, `time_consumption`,
  or `memory_consumption`
param pages: is an iterator to list the page numbers to GET
param status: must be one of `AC`, `WA`, `TLE`, `MLE`, `RE`, `CLE`, `OLE`, `IE`, `WJ`,
  `WR`, or `Judging`
param user_glob: is used as the value of `f.User` query parameter
---

functions.name: competitive_verifier.oj.verify.shlex2._msquote
documentation:
Quote a string for use as a command line argument in DOS or Windows.

On windows, before a command line argument becomes a char* in a
program's argv, it must be parsed by both cmd.exe, and by
CommandLineToArgvW.

If for_cmd is true, then this will quote the string so it will
be parsed correctly by cmd.exe and then by CommandLineToArgvW.

If for_cmd is false, then this will quote the string so it will
be parsed correctly when passed directly to CommandLineToArgvW.

For some strings there is no way to quote them so they will
parse correctly in both situations.

functions.name: competitive_verifier.oj.tools.utils.green
documentation:
green(s) color s with green.

This function exists to encapsulate the coloring methods only in utils.py.

red(s) color s with red.

This function exists to encapsulate the coloring methods only in utils.py.

functions.name: competitive_verifier.oj.tools.utils.get_default_command
documentation:
get_default_command returns a command to execute the default output of g++ or clang++. The value is basically `./a.out`, but `..exe` on Windows.

The type of return values must be `str` and must not be `pathlib.Path`, because the strings `./a.out` and `a.out` are different as commands but same as a path.

This function prints many logs and does some I/O.

build_match_function builds the function to compare actual outputs and expected outputs.

This function doesn't any I/O.

functions.name: competitive_verifier.oj.tools.test_command.run_checking_output
documentation:
run_checking_output executes matching of the actual output and the expected output.

This function has file I/O including the execution of the judge command.
